title: Cayley-graph equivalence of Bateson learning-level reachability classes on S_9
_fixture_purpose: Certifies that the tiered reachability classes of family [191] are exactly the connected components of nested undirected Cayley graphs on S_9 under stratified generator sets. Independently verifies the component sizes (24,24,24,8,1)/(72,8,1)/(81), cumulative sums-of-squares 1793/5249/6561, and non-cumulative differences 1712/3456/1312 matching [191] exactly.
source_attribution: Arthur Cayley, 'Desiderata and Suggestions No. 2: The Theory of Groups: Graphical Representation' (American Journal of Mathematics 1, 1878, pp. 174-176) — original definition of the Cayley graph of a group with respect to a generating set. Max Dehn, 'Über unendliche diskontinuierliche Gruppen' (1911) — word problem on Cayley graphs. QA formalization: Will Dale + Claude, 2026-04-11. Prerequisite: family [191] qa_bateson_learning_levels_cert.
theorem_statement: Let Cay_undirected(S_9, Gamma) denote the undirected graph with vertex set S_9 = {(b,e) : 1<=b,e<=9} and an edge {s, g(s)} for each s in S_9 and each g in Gamma. Define the tier generator sets Gamma_{L1} = {T}; Gamma_{L2a} = Gamma_{L1} U {scalar_k : k in (Z/9Z)*, k != 1} U {swap}; Gamma_{L2b} = Gamma_{L2a} U {scalar_k : gcd(k,9)>1, k!=1} U {const_(9,9)}. Then the connected components of Cay_undirected(S_9, Gamma_{tier}) equal the tier-reachability classes of [191] Bateson learning levels.
undirected_convention_note: Undirected Cayley graph is essential: scalar_3 and const_(9,9) are non-bijective on S_9 (kernels of size > 1), so reachability is only symmetric under closure of generators under inverses, which the standard Cayley-graph undirected convention provides.

correspondence_to_191.L1_components: T-orbits — three cosmos orbits of length 24, one satellite orbit of length 8, the singularity fixed point
correspondence_to_191.L2a_components: orbit families — cosmos (72 states), satellite (8 states), singularity (1 state)
correspondence_to_191.L2b_components: entire state space S_9 (81 states) — one connected component

witnesses.effect: T merges the trivial component {(1,1)} with {(1,2)}; repeated application traces out the 24-element cosmos orbit containing (1,1)
witnesses.note: Canonical QA step; L1 generator set {T} produces exactly the T-orbit decomposition (24+24+24+8+1 = 81)

witnesses.effect: scalar_2 is a (Z/9Z)* unit (order 6); it merges the three cosmos orbits into a single 72-element family component; satellite orbit is fixed setwise because 2*{3,6,9} = {6,3,9} mod 9
witnesses.note: After adding scalar_2, _4, _5, _7, _8 and swap, components become (72, 8, 1) = cosmos/satellite/singularity families

witnesses.effect: scalar_3 is a non-unit (gcd(3,9)=3), image is the satellite family; under undirected convention this merges cosmos and satellite components
witnesses.note: Adding scalar_3, scalar_6, and const_singularity collapses all three families into a single 81-element component

cross_references.relation: (Z/9Z)* is the 6-element cyclic group providing concrete L_2a generators via scalar multiplication

cross_references.relation: orbit classification via v_3(f) is the family invariant underlying the L_2a component structure

cross_references.relation: (Z/9Z)* cyclic structure dovetails with the Carmichael lambda(24)=2 extremality statement

verification_result: matches_191_counts = true; all three tier cumulative_pair_counts equal expected; all three non-cumulative differences equal [191] BATESON_191_S9_COUNTS
